name: The Greywater Stilts
descriptionSeed: A town built on stilts over the southern marsh by people who wanted out from under both cities — Adaptors, mostly, plus whoever else needed somewhere the aligned powers don't reach. Rope-walks lace between platforms; skiffs slide the channels below. They govern by a talking-circle that runs long and settles hard, and they're proud of owing nothing to the Heights or the Plateau. That independence is the whole story here: the Stilts are one bad season, one outside pressure, one internal fracture away from having to choose a side they swore they never would.
encounterFlavor: A community that runs itself and shows it — the talking-circle mid-argument, a marriage negotiated between platforms, a skiff-race, a newcomer being weighed for whether they'll take or give. Warm until it isn't.
questSeeds: Reed-Mother Ossa is dying and won't say so; the talking-circle has no agreed way to choose a successor and three factions are already maneuvering — an outsider could steady it or be used by it

A Heights envoy has come with a generous offer that would end the Stilts' independence in exchange for safety; the town is genuinely split and wants a clear-eyed outsider to argue the case they can't argue without fighting
Young Hollis wants to leave the marsh for the cities and their family is treating it as a betrayal; the real question underneath is whether the Stilts can survive its young people leaving
The ferryman Tam knows every channel and every secret; he's being quietly blackmailed over one of them, and the leverage would sink more than him
axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
worldPosNote: Near-centre folk country — the Valley sits where all axes cross faintly, so longitude spreads and colatitude stays low. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
